title: acer laurinum
intro: Acer laurinum, also known as the golden chain tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree with clusters of yellow flowers that are highly toxic and should be handled with caution. The plant contains saponins, which have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Acer laurinum have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Acer laurinum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although caution is advised due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
